verdicts.note: slack.com domain is referenced throughout evidence (pricing page, API docs).
Evidence describes Slack as a workspace collaboration/messaging platform.
Evidence confirms REST-style Web API, OAuth2/bearer tokens, and existing MCP server at github.com/korotovsky/slack-mcp-server.
oauth.v2.access method documentation confirms OAuth 2.0 flow for obtaining bearer tokens.
auth.test documentation and Web API docs confirm bearer token authentication via Authorization header or token parameter.
Pricing page for Free plan shows developers can create apps and integrate for free, supporting 'self-serve-free' classification.
Free plan page confirms free app development capability with limitations (3 apps on free plan, 90-day message history), aligning with notes about plan-dependent capabilities.
Web API documentation explicitly describes it as 'HTTP RPC-style methods' with REST-like characteristics.
Documentation references 'a collection of HTTP RPC-style methods' covering messaging, channels, users, files, billing, etc., supporting 'broad' classification.
Evidence confirms RPC-style HTTP API with hundreds of methods, Events API, and SDK support.
URL to github.com/korotovsky/slack-mcp-server is provided as evidence of existing MCP server.
Specific GitHub repository URL is provided in the record.
Evidence confirms OAuth2 authentication, broad API, and existing MCP server, supporting 'buildable-now' assessment.
Evidence does not reveal significant technical blockers; free plan supports app development and OAuth flows are documented.
All 5 evidence URLs are valid and support their claimed purposes.
0.75 confidence is reasonable given comprehensive API documentation and existing MCP server, though some nuances around ToS and admin approval aren't directly confirmed in evidence.

While flags mention session tokens violating ToS and admin approval requirements, the provided evidence does not directly confirm these specific claims. These are reasonable concerns but lack direct evidence.

revised.name: Slack
revised.one_liner: Slack provides a broad, self-serve REST-style Web API secured via OAuth2/bearer tokens, with existing community MCP servers making agent toolkits buildable today.

auth_methods.notes: Standard OAuth 2.0 flow (oauth.v2.access) to obtain bearer tokens scoped to a workspace; recommended approach for third-party apps.
Bearer tokens (including test/dev tokens) passed via Authorization header or token param; bot/user tokens generated via app configuration.

revised.self_serve_notes: Developers can create a Slack app and generate OAuth tokens for free on the Free plan to build/test integrations; some capabilities depend on the host workspace's plan (e.g., 3-app limit on free plan, 90-day history) and commercial distribution may require additional agreements/review.

api_surface.notes: Slack Web API is a broad RPC-style HTTP API with hundreds of methods for messaging, channels, users, files, etc. Also has Events API, RTM, and SDKs.

evidence.supports: Describes Web API as broad REST-like RPC API
Endpoint to check workspace plan programmatically

flags: Free plan has 3-app limit (not 10 as originally stated)
Admin approval may be required for some workspace installations
